task: I need to send a short note to a client about a delay and don’t want it to sound awkward. Can you draft a couple of versions for me, maybe one more polished and one more plain, so I can pick the better fit?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are two short options you can use:

**Polished:**  
Hi [Client Name], I wanted to let you know there’s been a slight delay on our side. We’re working to resolve it as quickly as possible and will keep you updated. Thanks for your patience and understanding.

**Plain:**  
Hi [Client Name], just a quick note that we’re running a bit behind. We’re on it and will update you as soon as we can. Thanks for bearing with us.

If you want, I can also make them sound more **formal, apologetic, or friendly** depending on the client.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s workable. The plain one sounds closer to how I’d say it. The polished one is fine too, just a little more polished than I usually need.
